about.hero_title: The New Era of Cosmetic Curation.
about.hero_desc: Careful Baza Labs is not just a brand. We are a global selection laboratory dedicated to finding the most advanced formulas in the world.
about.sec1_title: From the origin laboratory straight to your skin.
about.sec1_p1: The traditional beauty market is saturated with middlemen. Each step adds exorbitant profit margins that you pay for.
about.sec1_p2: At Careful Baza Labs, we redesigned consumer engineering. We test and approve only what delivers real clinical results.
about.pillar1_d: We seek excellence wherever it is, from Asian innovation to European purity.
about.pillar2_d: We reject 90% of the products we test. We analyze active ingredient concentration and the absence of toxins.
about.pillar3_d: Clinical-grade active ingredients with incomparable value in the global market.

skin.title: Definitive Guide: Skin Types
skin.subtitle: Understand your face's biology for a high-performance routine.
skin.intro: Identifying your skin type is the first step to stop wasting money on the wrong products. The skin is a living, dynamic organ, and its needs change. Below, we mapped out the most common profiles and the exact science to treat them.

oily.title: Oily Skin and Enlarged Pores
oily.body: Characterized by the overproduction of sebum by the sebaceous glands. Excessive shine appears a few hours after washing and pores become visible. The most common mistake is washing your face several times a day with strong soaps, which removes the natural protection and causes a "rebound effect".
oily.tip: Avoid thick beaded exfoliators. They create micro-lesions that inflame the pores. Opt for a gentle chemical exfoliation and ultra-light gel hydration.
oily.why: Niacinamide penetrates the pores and "tells" the gland to decrease oil production, while Zinc works by drying active breakouts.

dry.title: Dry Skin and Compromised Barrier
dry.body: Unlike dehydrated skin (which just needs water), dry skin doesn't produce enough lipids (natural oils). Without this natural "cement", the water you drink evaporates through your skin easily. It feels tight, rough, and flakes.
dry.tip: Never wash your face with hot shower water. The high temperature melts the few natural oils your skin managed to produce to defend itself.
dry.why: It combines Hyaluronic Acid (to pull water) and Ceramides (to lock that water inside), restoring comfort from the first use.

sensitive.title: Sensitive and Redness-prone Skin
sensitive.body: Sensitive skin is a condition of inflammation. The protective barrier is so thin that wind, cosmetics, and even stress penetrate easily, triggering alerts that cause redness and burning. The focus here is not to renew, but to calm.
sensitive.tip: Adopt minimalism (Skin Diet). When your skin is in crisis and burning, suspend acids and use only a gentle soap and a calming moisturizer.
sensitive.why: Its botanical prebiotics feed the "good bacteria" on your skin, creating a protective layer that instantly reduces redness.

ingredients.title: Clinical Dictionary of Actives
ingredients.subtitle: Total transparency about what really transforms your skin.
ingredients.intro: The cosmetic industry profits from empty promises and complicated names. At Careful Baza Labs, we translate the science so you know exactly what you're investing in.

niacinamide.title: Niacinamide (Vitamin B3)
niacinamide.body: It's the "jack of all trades" in skincare. It lightens dark spots, visually shrinks pores, and calms inflamed breakouts. Best of all? It doesn't irritate and can be used under the sun.
niacinamide.tip: Niacinamide serums mix with everything. You can pair it with Vitamin C in the morning or Retinol at night without fear of a bad reaction.
niacinamide.why: A clinical 10% dose guarantees visible results in 14 days for pores and uneven texture, with zero stinging.

retinol.title: Retinol (Vitamin A)
retinol.body: The only active proven by scientists to actually reverse aging. It enters the cell and tells it to "work faster", replacing old skin with new skin. It thins the top layer (giving glow) and thickens the bottom layer (stimulating collagen).
retinol.tip: If you are a Retinol beginner, use the "Sandwich" technique: Apply moisturizer, then Retinol, and then moisturizer over it again. This prevents peeling.
retinol.why: We envelope the active in nanocapsules. It enters the skin and only opens deep down, ensuring new collagen without making your face red.

hair.title: The Science of Trichology
hair.subtitle: Diagnosing the structural health of your hair.
hair.intro: Knowing if your hair is straight or curly is just aesthetics. To truly treat it, we need to look under a microscope and understand porosity (the damage level of the "layer" that protects the strand).

porous.title: Porous and Damaged Hair (With Frizz)
porous.body: Imagine a hair strand like a roof. In healthy hair, the shingles (cuticles) are tightly closed. In porous hair (due to heat styling, bleaching, or sun), the shingles are lifted. Water enters easily but evaporates in seconds, leaving the hair dry and frizzy.
porous.tip: Do the cup test: place a loose strand of hair in a cup of water. If it sinks quickly, it's porous (needs oil and reconstruction). If it floats on top, it has low porosity (only needs light hydration).
porous.why: Dense vegetable oils act as a "glue" for the hair's shingles, sealing the cuticles and preventing moisture from escaping throughout the day.

fine.title: Fine, Oily, and Flat Hair
fine.body: Fine hair is very delicate and has less "body". Because the strand is so smooth and light, the natural oil produced by the scalp slides to the ends very quickly, making the hair look "limp" on the same day it was washed.
fine.tip: The golden rule for fine hair: Conditioner should never touch above the mid-lengths. Apply only to the extreme ends so you don't weigh down the roots.
fine.why: Being 100% free of heavy silicones, it detangles fine hair, bringing softness without adding the weight of traditional creams.

tips.title: Routine Protocols
tips.subtitle: The engineering behind flawless skincare.
tips.intro: Buying expensive products and applying them in the wrong order throws money away. The universal rule of cosmetology is simple: always apply from the most liquid product to the thickest.

morning.title: The Morning Routine (Attack & Defend)
morning.body: The goal of the morning is not to treat severe problems, it's to protect your face from the sun, car smoke, and cell phone light. The correct protocol is: 1. Gentle Cleansing > 2. Antioxidant (e.g., Vitamin C) > 3. Light Moisturizer > 4. Sunscreen.
morning.tip: If your skin is normal to dry, you don't need to use soap in the morning! Just wash your face with cold water. This saves the hydration your skin built while you slept.
morning.why: Niacinamide acts as a shield that protects cells against the darkening of spots caused by daily radiation and pollution.

night.title: The Night Routine (Repair & Restore)
night.body: During deep sleep, your facial blood flow increases and your skin goes into "repair mode". This is the time to put the strong actives (like acids) to work. The order is: 1. Deep Cleansing > 2. Specific Treatment (Serums) > 3. Rich Moisturizing Cream.
night.tip: The Korean secret of Double Cleansing: Use an oil-based product first to melt makeup and sunscreen, and then wash with your facial gel soap to truly clean your pores.
night.why: Used as the last step at night, it creates an invisible "blanket" over the skin, preventing water and serums from evaporating while you sleep.
